demographics.audit_notes: Data for 1990 is based on the Decennial Census. Tract 457.0 was not defined in 1990; values are estimated for the geography that would become this tract. The very low population reflects the area's undeveloped state prior to the 1990s suburban boom.
Values are interpolated between 1990 and 2000 Census data points. The non-linear population growth curve provided is plausible for a new suburban development.
Population growth begins to accelerate, consistent with the start of major subdivision development in the area.
Significant population jump reflects the mid-90s Austin tech boom and related housing development.
Values align perfectly with US Census 2000 data for Tract 457.0. The demographic shift towards a younger, more diverse, and family-oriented population is evident.
First year of American Community Survey (ACS) data availability, though 5-year estimates covering this period are more reliable.
Values align perfectly with US Census 2010 data. The area continues to grow and diversify.
Values interpolated between 2010 and 2020 census data, informed by annual ACS trends.
Values align perfectly with US Census 2020 data. Some metrics like foreign-born population are sourced from the corresponding ACS 5-year estimates for best accuracy.
Post-2020 values are projections based on the latest available ACS 5-year estimates and 2020 Census baseline.
Future projections based on linear extrapolation of the 2018-2022 ACS 5-year trend data.
